content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

A sum of money placed at compound interest doubles itself in 3 years. In how many years will it amount to 8 times itself?

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

Let in 3 years, P = y, C.I = 2y (since, it doubles itself)
Now, by formula, 
C.I = P (1 + (R / 100))T
⇒ 2y = y (1 + (R / 100))3
⇒ 2 = (1 + (R / 100))3 

So, (1 + (R/100)) = 21/3      . . . (i)

We need to find, T = ? when C.I = 8y,
By formula,

C.I = P (1 + (R / 100))T   
⇒ 8y = y(21/3)T              [From equation (i)]
⇒ 23y = y(2T/3)
On Comparing, we get
T/3 = 3
⇒ T = 3(3)
⇒ T = 9 years.

Therefore, in 9 years the the sum of money will amount to 8times itself.
